title: Impairment
description: A single upstream dependency state change that affects delivery for one or more packages within a media buy. An impairment is open until the underlying resource returns to a serviceable state. Materiality rule: an impairment MUST list at least one package whose ability to serve is degraded; cosmetic-only effects (e.g., one rejected creative when peers remain serviceable in the same package) MUST NOT be reported as impairments.

impairment_id.description: Stable identifier for this impairment. Used as the notification_id when the impairment fires via webhook. Buyer agents dedupe by this value across at-least-once delivery. Remains stable for the lifetime of the open impairment (re-emission on snapshot reads carries the same id).

resource_type.description: The kind of upstream dependency that transitioned to an offline state.

resource_id.description: Seller's identifier for the specific resource that transitioned. References the same id space as the corresponding sync_/list_ task responses (e.g., audience_id, creative_id).

package_ids.description: Packages within this media buy whose delivery is degraded by the impairment. MUST list at least one package — cosmetic effects that do not degrade any package's ability to serve MUST NOT be reported as impairments.

transition.description: The resource-level status transition that triggered this impairment.

to.description: Current (offline) status of the resource (e.g., 'suspended', 'rejected', 'withdrawn', 'insufficient'). For property resource_type without a per-resource status field, set to a documented offline marker (e.g., 'depublished').

reason_code.description: Categorical reason for the offline transition. Drives buyer-side remediation logic.

reason.description: Human-readable explanation. Supplements reason_code with seller-specific detail.

observed_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the seller observed the resource transition to its offline state.

remediation.description: Action the buyer can take to clear the impairment, if any. Free text. Absent when no buyer-side remediation is possible (e.g., seller-initiated withdrawal pending re-publication).
